description:
An open-source alternative to Claude Cowork, powered by OpenCode

OpenWork is an extensible, open-source “Claude Work” style system for knowledge workers.
It’s a native desktop app that runs OpenCode under the hood, but presents it as a clean, guided workflow:
- pick a workspace
- start a run
- watch progress + plan updates
- approve permissions when needed
- reuse what works (templates + skills)
The goal: make “agentic work” feel like a product, not a terminal.
Highlights
- Preserve child task sessions when the workspace tree re-syncs, so nested task context stays visible instead of disappearing from the sidebar.
- Improve remote permission prompts by exposing owner tokens in the flow, making remote worker handoff and recovery easier to complete.
- Ship better share previews with HTML-first crawler links, refined OG skill cards, and full Japanese localization coverage.
Fixes & polish
- Allow removing the default skills hub repo when users want a fully custom skills setup.
- Refresh app, desktop, orchestrator, server, and router packages to 0.11.164 with the matching lockfile and release metadata.
- Clean up outdated README UI references and keep release stats current.
Packaging / ops
- Update AUR packaging for 0.11.163 as part of the release train and add a local amd64 Arch build runbook.
Compare:
https://github.com/different-ai/openwork/compare/v0.11.163...v0.11.164
